standard_by_standard_findings.no-inline-code: PASS. qa.md Phase 5 step 8 references the executable guard scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py instead of inlining vacuity logic (prescribed extract-to-script pattern). Fenced blocks are single-line invocation examples.
standard_by_standard_findings.no-hardcoded-values: PASS. repair-venv.sh parses pyvenv.cfg executable= via awk (77-90); venv resolved from script location (54-61) with --venv override. Only /usr/bin/python3 grep hit (line 7) is in the header comment documenting the rule. qa-manifest-guard.py inputs via stdin/argparse; no /root//home//tmp logic literals. See codex_consult for 3 robustness observations classified out-of-scope.
standard_by_standard_findings.use-source-venv: PASS. repair-venv.sh invokes the venv OWN resolved interpreter symlinks (the repaired/verified artifact), not bare system python3 (codex confirmed (a)). python3 grep hits are comments or the symlink-name loop (line 134). qa.md:1089 uses source-activate-then-python3 (codex confirmed (b)).
standard_by_standard_findings.integer-step-numbering: PASS. New Phase 5 steps numbered 6,7,8,9 (integers); step-8 sub-bullets are unnumbered bullets, not decimal sub-steps.
standard_by_standard_findings.meaningful-naming: PASS. qa-manifest-guard.py, repair-venv.sh use descriptive noun-noun/verb-noun names; no enhance-/fast-/-v2 patterns.
standard_by_standard_findings.english-only: PASS. Scripts English-only (zero CJK in scripts/). Unconditional agents/+commands/ grep found CJK only in agents/style-inspector.md (this file, pre-existing, out of diff) - none in qa.md. Sole diff CJK hit is test_AC3:79, a regex alternation arm matching qa.md forbid-phrasing; allowlisted test-fixture literal; qa.md uses English MUST NOT (codex confirmed).
standard_by_standard_findings.merge-similar-scripts: PASS. Both scripts net-new with no pre-existing sibling/equivalent (verified against scripts/ inventory).
standard_by_standard_findings.concise-documentation: PASS. Script docstrings are functional contracts; qa.md edits are rule/contract text. No marketing/story prose.
standard_by_standard_findings.parameterized-scripts: PASS. repair-venv.sh: --venv + script-location default + runtime pyvenv.cfg parse. qa-manifest-guard.py: stdin + --cycle-diff-files + --collect-only-cmd. No run-variable hardcoded.
standard_by_standard_findings.dangling-references: PASS. qa.md ref scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py exists (Glob). guard imports stdlib only. AC tests ref both scripts which exist.
standard_by_standard_findings.no-merge-conflicts: PASS. Conflict-marker grep across both scripts + AC tests returned zero (count grep = 0).

introduced_in_diff_summary: All audited files are introduced_in_diff:true. No line-level findings emitted, so diff-hunk-overlap + pre-diff-baseline newness test is vacuously satisfied. The one CJK occurrence (test_AC3:79) classified as allowlisted test-fixture regex literal, not a new violation.
verdict: PASS - no style violations introduced by this cycle's diff. The two python-invocation patterns are correctly compliant; the sole diff CJK token is an allowlisted test-fixture regex literal. Confidence: high - both new scripts fully read against all 11 standards, qa.md Phase 5 + schema docblock fully read, grep-based standards returned concrete clean results, and codex adversarial review surfaced only out-of-scope code-quality observations.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) returned 3 REFUTE + 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY. The 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY confirmed the verdict (use-source-venv classifications (a)/(b) and the CJK test-fixture allowlist). The 3 REFUTE findings are real code-quality/robustness observations outside the 11 style standards; caller-filtered to out_of_scope per codex protocol Rule 3 (finding count is information, not verdict).

findings.proposed_fix: Add cycle-diff-only --collect-timeout-seconds argparse option (default 60) passed into _collect_one_file().
findings.rationale: Standard 2 allowlist exempts constants that never change and defaults-with-fallback. A 60s defensive timeout is an internal constant, not an env-specific value breaking portability. Optional-configurability enhancement (in_scope_minor, future cycle); not a no-hardcoded-values/parameterized-scripts FAIL.

findings.proposed_fix: Derive INTERPRETER_NAME=$(basename ${INTERPRETER}); build deduped target list python,python3,${INTERPRETER_NAME}.
findings.rationale: python3.12 is a venv-structural symlink NAME (venv literally has bin/python3.12), not a /usr/bin path literal; interpreter TARGET is parsed from pyvenv.cfg. BA AC2 enumerates these exact three names. Portability improvement for non-3.12 venvs (in_scope_minor robustness); not one of the 11 style standards.

findings.proposed_fix: Resolve ${VENV} absolute and compare to realpath(sys.prefix) via env/argv.
findings.rationale: Runtime-logic correctness (health-probe under --venv override) = QA/dev regression scope, NOT style cleanliness scope per spec-20260503-091826 scope contract. Maps to no style standard.
Codex agrees not a use-source-venv violation.

findings.issue: test_AC3 CJK regex token under english-only.
findings.rationale: Codex agrees generated test-fixture literal; qa.md uses English MUST NOT; not a violation.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY confirmed the inspector's contested classifications, strengthening the clean verdict. All 3 REFUTE caller-filtered to out_of_scope (legitimate robustness items, none mapping to the 11 style standards) and logged for a possible future cycle per codex protocol Rule 3/Rule 4.
